  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which primary focus should I apply to this paragraph: (A) sentence-level writing polish and clarity; (B) conceptual framing/novelty and overclaim risk (assessing “first framework”/“deployment-ready” scope); or (C) logical consistency of the method description and estimand definitions? You can select one main focus and optionally a second.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "This paragraph advances a thesis and contribution, so reframing the gap and positioning above the sentence level is central.",
    "R1.2": "The chain from ambiguous treatment definitions to version-specific ATEs and a coverage-controlled set should be checked for internal logical coherence.",
    "R1.4": "Because it asserts being the “first framework” and an “end-to-end procedure,” assessing the claimed novelty’s scope is essential without a full literature audit.",
    "R1.6": "Given the request targets a short passage, sentence-level clarity, concision, and tone are likely the most impactful improvements.",
    "RX.1": "Guidance should explicitly acknowledge uncertainties and assumptions (e.g., identification and regularity conditions) to avoid overstated certainty.",
    "RX.2": "The projection must emphasize restraint on absolute claims like “deployment-ready” or universal estimator agnosticism unless tightly scoped.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain confined to this paragraph and not extend to broader manuscript restructuring or new analyses.",
    "RX.4": "Recommendations should avoid encouraging overconfident policy conclusions or misuse of causal estimands.",
    "RX.5": "Any feedback should tie observations to specific phrases in the provided text and avoid unverifiable external assertions."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which primary focus should I apply to this paragraph: (A) sentence-level writing polish and clarity; (B) conceptual framing/novelty and overclaim risk (assessing “first framework”/“deployment-ready” scope); or (C) logical consistency of the method description and estimand definitions? You can select one main focus and optionally a second.

rationale.R1.1: This paragraph advances a thesis and contribution, so reframing the gap and positioning above the sentence level is central.
rationale.R1.2: The chain from ambiguous treatment definitions to version-specific ATEs and a coverage-controlled set should be checked for internal logical coherence.
rationale.R1.4: Because it asserts being the “first framework” and an “end-to-end procedure,” assessing the claimed novelty’s scope is essential without a full literature audit.
rationale.R1.6: Given the request targets a short passage, sentence-level clarity, concision, and tone are likely the most impactful improvements.
rationale.RX.1: Guidance should explicitly acknowledge uncertainties and assumptions (e.g., identification and regularity conditions) to avoid overstated certainty.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must emphasize restraint on absolute claims like “deployment-ready” or universal estimator agnosticism unless tightly scoped.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain confined to this paragraph and not extend to broader manuscript restructuring or new analyses.
rationale.RX.4: Recommendations should avoid encouraging overconfident policy conclusions or misuse of causal estimands.
rationale.RX.5: Any feedback should tie observations to specific phrases in the provided text and avoid unverifiable external assertions.
